codeReview.summary: The feature adds the requested heuristics, cached PST state, and evaluation tests, but it establishes a white-centric score contract while the engine's negamax search consumes `eval_evaluate()` directly as a side-to-move static eval. That sign mismatch makes search, razoring, and null-move logic behave incorrectly on black-to-move nodes, and the new tests reinforce the wrong contract instead of catching it.

issues.description: `eval_evaluate()` accumulates material/PST/pawn/activity/king-safety terms as white-minus-black (`lines 730-745`) and only tweaks the result with a tempo bonus (`line 747`), so the score stays positive for White even when Black is to move. A direct probe of `4k3/8/8/8/8/8/3Q4/4K3` returns `1034` with `w` and `1014` with `b`. The search layer consumes `eval_evaluate()` directly as `static_eval` inside negamax/quiescence (`engine/src/search.c:349,495`) before applying `score = -search_negamax(...)` on child nodes (`engine/src/search.c:530,645-656`), which requires side-to-move scores. As written, losing black-to-move nodes are searched with the wrong sign, corrupting pruning thresholds and move selection.

The new symmetry test locks in the white-centric contract by expecting `white_knight_score + black_knight_score` to be near zero when the mirrored position is evaluated with `b` to move (`lines 176-181`). There is still no regression test covering the search-facing contract: a materially losing black-to-move position should either evaluate negative after normalization or use a dedicated side-to-move helper, so the blocking sign mismatch above currently goes uncaught.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `engine-worker` skill is out of sync with the repository layout and native test command. It still tells workers to inspect `engine/` and `tests/` and compile `engine/*.c tests/*.c`, while this repo and mission guidance use `engine/src/*.c` and `engine/tests/*.c`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/engine-worker/SKILL.md:35-36,42,63,124,129,141` still references `engine/` + `tests/`; mission guidance `/Users/aravhawk/.factory/missions/70ca98e6-cb3b-4e1c-9eb9-4d887ed17139/AGENTS.md:39,45-46,58` and the transcript skeleton show the worker actually had to use `engine/src/` and `engine/tests/`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared legality note no longer describes the full make/unmake state that must round-trip exactly. After this feature, `Position` also carries incremental `pawn_hash` and `pst_mg/pst_eg` evaluation caches, but the library note still only calls out castling/en-passant/clocks/Zobrist restoration.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/library/engine-legality.md:6` mentions only castling rights, en passant, clocks, and Zobrist hashes; `engine/src/position.h:17-18,27-35` now stores `pawn_hash` plus `pst_mg/pst_eg`, and `engine/tests/test_legality.c:79-82` was extended to verify those PST caches are restored too.

summary: Reviewed commit `b644770beb2ef045822804bcaabfc253764e292e`, the handoff, and the worker transcript skeleton for `engine-evaluation`. The implementation adds substantial evaluation functionality, but scrutiny should fail because the exported evaluation score is white-centric while the engine's negamax search consumes it as a side-to-move static eval, producing incorrect signs on black-to-move nodes.
